subtasks.description: Orchestrate retrieval of project requirements from Jira, check team availability via calendar API, schedule standup meeting, and share agenda via Slack. This requires coordinating multiple tool invocations across the enterprise tool registry (Jira, Calendar, Meeting Scheduler, Slack).

plan.cost_rationale: Single subtask using multitool_plan because the query explicitly requests a multi-step tool orchestration workflow involving four distinct tools from the enterprise registry (Jira, Calendar API, Meeting Scheduler, Slack). This is the canonical use case for multitool_plan. Assigned mid tier because the orchestration involves sequential dependencies (requirements → availability → scheduling → notification) and requires conditional logic to check availability before scheduling. A single mid-tier multitool_plan agent can generate the complete tool-call sequence with proper dependency handling and error handling paths, eliminating the need to decompose into separate sql_gen, mongo_query, or other specialist tasks (none of which apply here since this is pure tool orchestration, not data querying).

arguments.title: Team Standup Meeting
arguments.description: Daily standup to discuss project requirements and blockers. Agenda: $1.results

arguments.details: Standup meeting created with project requirements from Jira

arguments.message:
📅 **Team Standup Meeting Scheduled**

**Time:** 2024-01-01 09:00 AM
**Duration:** 30 minutes
**Location:** Virtual

**Agenda - Project Requirements:**
$1.results

**Team Availability:** $2.results

**Meeting Link:** $3.calendar_link

Please review the requirements and come prepared to discuss!
